strategic_profile.executive_summary: Stevens Institute of Technology is a premier private research university located in Hoboken, New Jersey, directly across the river from New York City. The institution is renowned for its technology-centric curriculum, rigorous STEM degrees, and a strong emphasis on experiential learning, including cooperative education and industry internships.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Stevens features a heavily STEM-oriented curriculum where even business and arts degrees incorporate strong analytical and technological requirements.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants must demonstrate high quantitative proficiency and readiness for rigorous coursework, particularly in mathematics and physical sciences.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The university consistently ranks among the top in the nation for early-career salaries and ROI, with median earnings exceeding $112,000 ten years post-enrollment.
analyst_takeaways.implication: While the initial cost of attendance is high (approx. $84,906/year), the long-term return on investment justifies the expense for many graduates, particularly in fields like Computer Science and Quantitative Finance.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Stevens benefits immensely from its proximity to Manhattan, which drives both its social dynamics and highly successful pre-professional internship and co-op pipelines.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students seeking a traditional, isolated residential college experience might find the campus small, but those eager for urban integration and early career networking will thrive.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: A smaller, 55-acre urban campus overlooking the Manhattan skyline. The social environment is heavily influenced by its rigorous STEM culture, active Greek life, and the ease of accessing New York City for nightlife and entertainment.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Hoboken offers excellent public transit (PATH train, ferries, NJ Transit), providing students with seamless 10-minute access to Manhattan, profoundly enhancing internship and career opportunities.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Classified as an R2 Doctoral University with high research activity. Undergraduates are highly encouraged to participate in cutting-edge research across disciplines such as biomedical engineering, quantum physics, and artificial intelligence.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional for general admissions; however, submitting competitive scores is common and may be beneficial for highly competitive majors or merit scholarship consideration.

gpa.notes: Most incoming freshmen graduate in the top 10 percent of their high school class.

colleges.name: Schaefer School of Engineering and Science
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for analytically driven students seeking rigorous STEM preparation, robust cooperative education experiences, and high early-career earning potential.
colleges.housing_profile: Freshmen are guaranteed housing, but upperclassmen often shift to a lottery system or seek off-campus apartments in Hoboken.

core_courses: CS 146 Introduction to Web Programming and Project Development
ENGR 111 Introduction to Engineering Design & Systems Thinking
ENGR 116 Intro to Programming & Algorithmic Thinking

colleges.name: School of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking to blend foundational business skills with emerging technological tools, including data analytics and financial engineering.

core_courses: QF 101 Introduction to Quantitative Finance I
QF 102 Introduction to Quantitative Finance II
QF 202 Introduction to Financial Time Series
QF 212 Adv Probability and Stochastic Processes for QF
QF 301 Advanced Time Series Analytics and Machine Learning
QF 302 Financial Market Microstructure and Trading
QF 435 Risk Management for Capital Mkts

supplemental_requirements.details: Personal statement required via Common App.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Stevens places a strong emphasis on quantitative preparation; applicants to engineering and computer science programs are expected to show high achievement in advanced mathematics and physical sciences.

application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Applicants are allowed to select a second/alternative major on their application. If an applicant is not competitive for an impacted first-choice major (like Computer Science), they will be reviewed for their secondary choice, making strategic selection of the alternate major crucial.

scholarships.name: Ann P. Neupauer Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers full tuition and is renewable for eight semesters for top students in engineering, math, or computer science.

scholarships.name: Edwin A. Stevens Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Awarded for demonstrated excellence in high school academics.

scholarships.name: FIRST Robotics Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Renewable for four years for students who participated on a FIRST team during high school.

scholarships.name: The Stevens Investment
scholarships.benefits: Beginning Fall 2026, Stevens covers full tuition for qualifying first-time, full-time undergraduate students from families earning $75,000 or less.

ap_policy.general_rule: Stevens recognizes most AP courses with a score of 4 or 5.
ap_policy.usage: Incoming freshmen may utilize a maximum total of 30 credits combined from AP, IB, and dual-enrollment college coursework.

ib_policy.general_rule: Grants credit for specific Higher Level (HL) exams with qualifying scores.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: A maximum of 30 advanced standing credits (AP/IB/Transfer) can be applied to an undergraduate degree for incoming freshmen.

what_it_takes: Rigorous high school STEM curriculum, particularly Calculus and Physics.
High GPA (average 3.88) with near-perfect grades in math/science.
Demonstrated interest in technological innovation or robotics.
essay_tips: Connect personal goals clearly to Stevens' focus on innovation, cooperative education, and technological impact.
red_flags: Dropping STEM courses in senior year.
insights: Stevens is highly regarded for its Co-op program, which significantly boosts employability.
The gender ratio is historically skewed toward men, though the university actively recruits to diversify.
